title: GitHub makes the redesigned Copilot CLI terminal interface generally available
friendlyTitle: GitHub Copilot CLI gets a full terminal workspace
summary: GitHub made its redesigned Copilot CLI terminal interface generally available, adding tabs for sessions, issues, pull requests, and gists, plus in-terminal setup for MCP servers, skills, plugins, themes, and settings. The new interface also adds accessibility improvements and lets users browse GitHub work without leaving the terminal.
friendlySummary: GitHub’s Copilot CLI now has tabs, issue and pull request browsing, easier setup for tools, and better accessibility.
whyItMatters: Coding agents are moving closer to the developer’s normal workspace instead of living in separate chat windows. A terminal UI that can browse issues, install MCP tools, and configure skills lowers the friction of using agents on real repositories.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Developers can use AI help without switching between many apps. That can make agent workflows feel more like normal terminal work.
